channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Fox News hires president's daughter-in-law Lara Trump for weekend show on network
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fox News Channel has hired the president's daughter-in-law, Lara Trump, to be host of a new weekend show that will debut later this month.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday intended to ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US officials now say Trump only wants to displace Palestinians from Gaza temporarily
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump’s top diplomat and his main spokesperson on Wednesday walked back the idea that he wants the permanent relocation of Palestinians from Gaza, after American allies and even Republican lawmakers rebuffed his suggestion that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Protesters in Bangladesh storm and destroy a house linked to exiled former Prime Minister Hasina
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Thousands of protesters in Bangladesh took out their anger at exiled former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina on Wednesday by destroying a family home that came to symbolize the country's independence — and now, they say, the authoritarianism they believe she led.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands protest Trump administration policies in cities across the US
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. on Wednesday to protest the Trump administration's early actions, decrying everything from the president's immigration crackdown to his rollback of transgender rights and a proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinians from the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump to sign executive order barring transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KTLA 5

item.title: Suspect arrested after one dead, 5 injured in New Albany mass shooting
item.description: For the latest updates in New Albany warehouse shooting, follow this link. NEW ALBANY, Ohio (WCMH) -- A man is in custody after one person was shot dead and five others were injured after a shooting Tuesday night at a New Albany warehouse. According to authorities, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, KTLA 5

item.title: CEO son of 'Silicon Valley pioneer' arrested in alleged San Francisco attack
item.description: A Silicon Valley CEO was arrested on Saturday in downtown San Francisco on suspicion of rape and other charges including robbery, assault with a deadly weapon, and false imprisonment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Artist explains how New Orleans childhood inspired Super Bowl logo
item.description: Months before the Chiefs and Eagles earned a ticket to Super Bowl LIX, the NFL design team asked Tajh to create both theme art and the logo for Super Bowl 59.
item.dc:creator: Amy Russo, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma board denies clemency for man on death row, clears path for March execution
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma board rejected clemency on Wednesday for a man sentenced to die for fatally shooting a woman during a 2005 home-invasion robbery, clearing the way for him to become the first person executed in the state this year.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rare invasive bug intercepted at Detroit airport
item.description: Agriculture specialists with U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirm that rare invasive seed bugs were intercepted on undeclared cargo late last year at Detroit Metro Airport.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Authorities nab White House fence climber just two weeks into Trump's new term
item.description: U.S. Secret Service took someone into custody after the individual sought to scale the White House fence on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Crews save unresponsive man in spinning boat on Florida lake
item.description: A man found unresponsive inside a boat that was driving in circles was saved by Florida authorities on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin officials need hunters' help to fight chronic wasting disease
item.description: After a deer tested positive for chronic wasting disease in Manitowoc County, officials are calling on hunters to help.
item.dc:creator: Paul Steeno, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi sworn in as attorney general in Oval Office
item.description: Pam Bondi was sworn in as attorney general on Wednesday in the Oval Office with President Trump looking on and telling reporters she would be “as impartial as a person can be." “I know I'm supposed to say, ‘She's going to be totally impartial with respect to Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mahomes reveals thoughts on Trump attending the Super Bowl
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes was asked what it will feel like to play in front of a sitting president.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: Judge cements block on Trump’s order restricting birthright citizenship
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — A federal judge Wednesday furthered a block of President Trump’s executive order preventing the children of migrants without legal status from receiving birthright citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: In bid for Davos-like gathering, Semafor plans to bring at least 200 CEOs to Washington conference
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The media company Semafor is planning a Washington economic conference for global CEOs this April that it hopes will rival the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, for influence.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 accused in Aurora kidnapping, torture appear in court
item.description: Three men accused of kidnapping and torturing a couple at an Aurora apartment complex were in court Monday.
item.dc:creator: Vicente Arenas, NewsNation

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Dems ask Hegseth to probe potential price gouging on military families' housing
item.description: “The Department of Defense has a responsibility to protect military families from predatory private housing companies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Shutdown fears grow as Trump battles multiply
item.description: The odds of a government shutdown are surging as President Trump battles Democrats over efforts to freeze funding and Republicans brawl internally over the size and scope of potential cuts.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Celebrities rail against Trump's policies
item.description: After years of launching blistering attacks on President Donald Trump, much of Hollywood appears to be casting him in a new "Harry Potter"-inspired role: He Who Must Not Be Named.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors who pushed Menendez brothers resentencing plan to sue new DA for ‘retaliation,’ alleged demotions
item.description: Two Los Angeles County prosecutors who advocated for the Menendez brothers' release from prison claim they were retaliated against by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID is going away. Here's what it's been doing in South America
item.description: The dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development will deliver a major blow to efforts including humanitarian assistance in Colombia, conservation efforts in Brazil and coca eradication in Peru — South American countries that have been a priority for the support.
item.dc:creator: Fabiano Maisonnave, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in California
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk shocks lawmakers, setting himself on collision course
item.description: Billionaire businessman Elon Musk is on a collision course with lawmakers on Capitol Hill who are starting to challenge his authority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Protests against Trump, Project 2025 planned in cities across the country
item.description: A movement to oppose the early actions of President Donald Trump’s administration is taking off online, with plans to protest across the U.S. on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Church vandalized by Proud Boys now controls group's trademark, judge rules
item.description: The Proud Boys is barred from selling merchandise using its name or symbols without permission from a Black church once vandalized by the right-wing extremist group's members, after a Washington, D.C., judge on Monday revoked its control over the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9
